cards.front: I'm sick so I can't come.
cards.grammarNote: 아프다 (to be sick) → stem 아프 → 아파서. When the stem ends in 으 before 아/어, the 으 drops: 아프 + 아서 → 아파서. Result: I'm sick, so I can't come.

cards.front: My head hurts so I need medicine.
cards.grammarNote: 아파서 links cause (head hurts) to result (need medicine). -아서/어서 cannot be used with past tense endings — the tense is shown in the final verb.

cards.front: I'm tired so I want to sleep.
cards.grammarNote: 피곤하다 (to be tired) → 피곤해서 (하다 verbs: 하 + 여서 → 해서). 자고 싶어요 = want to sleep.

cards.front: I had a fever so I stayed home.
cards.grammarNote: 나다 (to come out/occur) → stem 나 + 아서 → 나서. 열이 나다 = to have a fever. Note: tense marker (-었어요) only on the final verb.

cards.front: It was cold so I wore a jacket.
cards.grammarNote: 춥다 (to be cold) is irregular: 춥 → 추워서 (ㅂ irregular: ㅂ → 워 before vowels).

cards.front: My stomach hurts so I can't eat.
cards.grammarNote: 배가 아프다 = stomach hurts. 아파서 = because it hurts. 못 먹어요 = can't eat.

cards.front: I feel better so I'm going out.
cards.grammarNote: 나아지다 (to get better/improve) → 나아져서. 나가다 = to go out.

cards.front: I was dizzy so I sat down.
cards.grammarNote: 어지럽다 (to be dizzy) → ㅂ irregular: 어지러워서. 앉다 = to sit → 앉았어요 (past).

cards.front: My throat is sore so I'm drinking water.
cards.grammarNote: 목이 아프다 = throat hurts. 아파서 = because it hurts. Direct cause-and-result chain.

cards.front: My arm is broken so I can't write.
cards.grammarNote: 골절되다 (to be fractured) → 골절돼서 (되 + 어서 → 돼서). 쓸 수 없어요 = cannot write.

cards.front: I rested so I feel better.
cards.grammarNote: 쉬다 (to rest) → stem 쉬 + 어서 → 쉬어서. 나아지다 = to improve/get better. 몸 = body.

cards.front: I keep sneezing so I think I have a cold.
cards.back: 계속 재채기가 나서 감기인 것 같아요.
cards.romanized: Gyesok jaechaegiga naseo gamgiin geot gatayo.
cards.grammarNote: 계속 재채기가 나다 = to keep sneezing. 나서 = because [it] comes out. 감기 = cold. 인 것 같아요 = seems to be.
